behavior_summary: This script implements the object's U8P_MAGARMS::look/examine handler: when the player inspects the item, it shows a short descriptive text and then yields control back to the engine.
There are no conditionals, flags, or state changes; the behavior is a single, hardcoded presentation of the string "magic arm guards " followed by a suspend/return operation.
Item metadata (for example an armourClass value) exists in the file metadata but is not read or modified by this script.
notable_control_flow: U8P_MAGARMS::look executes as a process-type handler and performs a straight-line sequence: call Bark (display text) -> suspend (return control).
No branching, loops, menu presentation, or flag checks are present.
open_questions: Is the trailing space in the displayed string "magic arm guards " intentional or an artifact of the disassembly?
Is the armourClass / other item properties carried in the file used elsewhere by gameplay systems even though this script does not reference them?
Are there other processes or external scripts that respond to this item (use/destroy) not captured here?

main_function.name: U8P_MAGARMS::look
main_function.purpose: Provide the short player-facing description when the player examines the item (display a bark) and then suspend/return control to the engine.
entry_conditions: Invoked by the engine when the player performs the 'U8P_MAGARMS::look' / examine action on this object (processType 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_MAGARMS::look
function_map.role: U8P_MAGARMS::look/examine handler that displays the object's description

external_calls.purpose: Display the short descriptive text "magic arm guards " to the player.
Yield or terminate the U8P_MAGARMS::look process to return control to the engine (inferred engine control operation).

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: This script implements the object's U8P_MAGARMS::look/examine handler: when the player inspects the item, it shows a short descriptive text and then yields control back to the engine.
